BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Back-of-napkin check on whether the side jobs clear enough after fuel, materials, and the tax set-aside to be worth the weekend hours. Just for me and Brian's budget talk.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Solo work, no employees and no shop rent. Revisit once the downtown rewiring wraps and weekends free up.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Slow winter month - one fewer service call
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed monthly overhead about $100. At roughly $170 net per service call, one average call covers the overhead. Everything past that is real money toward the $20k savings target.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Even a slow month stays well in the black.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Liability insurance bumps at renewal
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. A modest insurance increase barely moves the break-even.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Side work clears about $500 a month to the household after costs and the tax set-aside. Worth it as long as it does not eat the soccer Saturdays. Revisit if fuel or insurance jumps.
ActionItems: Keep holding 25 percent of side income in the tax set-aside account
Re-check after the downtown rewiring project wraps and weekends free up
